Always let you tank go first and you go second. You will have an advantage in battle.Use lancelot ultimate to finish of the enemy.You can also run away from the enemy with your ultimate

emblems.emblem_desc: I use  swift to get extra attack speed so that i can farm faster during early game.Next, i use seasoned hunter in order to deal 15 perecent more damage to the lord and turtle. But it doesnt have effects on regular creeps.Finally, I use impure rage to deal 4 perecent more damage of the target max hp. It also resotres 2 perecent mana on hit.

equips.equip_desc: I use this build as it has great damage and a solid defense.The key item in this build is the war axe as it does a good amount of damage to enemy during early game and i also gives a not bad hp

hero.hero_overview: Lancelot is a hp in mlbb. He uses a sword to fight.I suggest getting the buff on the turtle site first.Then, rotate to gold lane.Lancelot is a powerful assassin who can quickly get out from battles if needed. He can chase enemies who are trying to run away easily too.
hero.hero_strength: Always start with the buff in turtle side so that you can rotate to goldlane and maybe kill the enemy marksman if you can.Never try to take the turtle alone especially in early game because Lancelot is very fragile in early game. So, you should put atleast 1 defense item on lancelot.When you are killing red buff or blue buff, you should always stack lancelot passive so that you can deal more damage. Try hunting feeble marksman or mages since they are easy to kill with lancelot during early or mid game. In late game, never fight a group of enemies alone. Wait for your teamates. Always ban the heroes who can counter you abilities. Talking abiout abilities, use your second skill to doge an enemy's attack. You can also use the ultimate.

hero.hero_overview:
Lancelot the Swordsmaster. 
One of the best junglers in the game and mostly used by professional players to dominate and carry the game. 
With lancelot's good clutch retri potential and fast movements(dashes) the enemies gets a hard time dealing with this hero.
hero.hero_strength:
Early Game
In the early game in this current meta lancelot can dominate every hero and he is fast at farming jungle creeps. 

Mid Game
In the mid game he can be unstoppable with 3-4 items and much more likely snowball lancelot cant be killable and he can easily burst any hero. 

Late Game
In the late game he can do many clutch retri because of his steal potential but itll be hard to finish enemies because theyre gonna be full item but its still manageable
hero.hero_weakness: His weakness are those who have annoying crowd controls and those who have so fast mobilities.

data.title: GODDESS OF WARS

cooperates.cooperate_desc:
Atlas or Tigreal grabs enemies together, allow Lancelot for dash at least 4 times with ease when they are being frozen or stunned.

Atlas and Tigreal often dives far from combat, and Lancelot can easily keep up the pace.

counters.counter_desc: Jawhead is very squishy against Lancelot. When Jawhead dives in, Lancelot can bait him by dashing along the minions, Flicker, ultimate until the turret and when Jawhead arrives, he just uses 2nd skill to dodge Jawhead's ultimate.

dominants.dominant_desc:
The minions give you free passive stack, so you better off wait for them.

Don't go first, aim for squishy heroes like marksman, assassin or mage.

Use the crab to get a free stack, so that you just need to dash 3 more times. The crab also triggers War Axe 4 stacks, then you just wait for 2 more seconds and get max stacks.

If your team has Granger or Chang'e, tell them to reserve their skills and use basic attacks only to clear minions, so that you can spam dash and clear minions after fight.

During the Screen Loading, take notes of enemies' battle spells like Petrify.

Eudora or Ruby are strong counters, while enemies like Minsitthar or Aurora have strong cc but can be maneuvered around.

Always dash at least two times with first skill before using 2nd skill for max damage. In desperation, you can dash the second time to a dashed unit or to the air.

Always gauge 2nd skill at the center, or use it to evade cc or Turret's damage.

Avoid initiating with Ultimate, as you'll be vulnerable after the 1st skill goes on cooldown on mistakes. The ultimate should be used as situational burst damage or repositioning.

Clear wave:
1(to the minion) - 1(back again) -2
1(up to 4 times) - 2 - 1(back)

Always aim your 1st skill, don't just click on it.

Use 1st on the minion or crab first, then use Barrel to enter amid enemies to dash a few more times before using 2nd skill and escape with ultimate.

Use 1st skill to dive in and 2nd skill to burst, use Barrel to escape out.

Keep in mind that Minsitthar's ult also disables your Barrel, and you cannot have two Barrels at once.
Don't fight persistently, wait when enemy heroes stick together. The downtime between fights allow Lancelot to heal back up, restore mana and cooldown of all abilities, or waiting for his allies.

Don't buy Boots for Lancelot, because if he can wipe out enemies in a swoop, there's no need to walk while he can dash. If he misses a dash from 2nd skill or crowd control, he cannot even run back to team.

Many people buy mana boots for Lancelot, but if his team has upperhand, they can always reach for the healing rune, each restores 50% max mana. If his team does not, he will constantly revive at base with full mana.

Use 2nd skill without any dash will deal less damage, while it is harder to deal damage with 1st skill afterwards or trigger Lethal Ignition. Even your basic attacks benefit from your passive.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Assassin emblem ensures max damage output and penetration.

Thrill gives more damage.

Lancelot has sufficient damage with Seasoned Hunter, so you can replace it with Bargain Hunter to get items quickly, pairs well with his frequent kill trading style.

Lethal Ignition can be activated easily and sync with combo interval.

equips.equip_desc:
You can buy these items with 2500 starting gold. Sky Piercer ensures some kills for items. Fury Hammer gives optimal physical damage and PEN.

Lancelot does not rely much on mana, but if game goes tough, the mana will be restored during downtime waiting for your allies respawning.

Fury Hammer should be upgraded into Hunter Strike, as it reduces cooldown of his 1st skill and 3rd skill when they go on cooldown unintentionally. 

Hunter Strike will slow enemies, allow you for more accurate 2nd skill on desired victim while Heptaseas only gives True Damage on the first dash.

War Axe gives some true damage when enemies notice your kill and buy armor. It also gives some lifesteal.
Most of the time your game will end before you have these items. Always look for healing enemies and counter them with Sea Halberd, while Malefic Roar is the ultimate penetration.

Sell Sky for BoD to clear wave faster, it also gives more damage than execution threshold 

Rosegold gives good survivability during dash.

This kit gives 20% cooldown reduction, 12% spell vamp and 10% lifesteal
This build gives you 30% cooldown reduction and 2 survival items: Queen's Wings and Rose Gold and 2 slow items: Thunderbelt and Hunter Strike.
equips.equip_title: Semi Build When enemies revenge on you

Out of meta in Rank, but Lancelot finds joy and his flamboyance in the chaotic nature of Brawl. The optimal playstyle in Brawl is vastly differently from how he is played in Rank.

This guide also suits for solo players.
hero.hero_strength:
High mobility
Low cooldown
Mana independent
Exploit the bustling nature of Brawl

spell.spell_desc: Purify allows you to complete your dash combo. In Brawl, Lancelot has bonus damage and never run into Damage issue, but the chain stun from your enemies. It also has short cooldown.

hero.hero_overview: Lancelot is one of the heroes in the game **Mobile Legends: Bang Bang**, and is known for being a fast and flexible fighter with high mobility and the ability to deal damage to enemies. Lancelot features an elegant design inspired by European knights, and carries a long sword that reflects his unique fighting skills.
hero.hero_strength:
1. **Fast Movement and Flexibility:** 
Lancelot has excellent movement abilities, as he can jump and move quickly between enemies using his skills, making him difficult to predict and counter. 

2. **High Damage:** 
He has the ability to deal great damage in a short time, especially when using his skills consecutively (combo). 

3. **Immunity:** 
One of Lancelot's skills gives him temporary immunity to attacks, making him able to avoid damage at critical moments. 

4. **Penetration Ability:** 
He can easily penetrate enemy ranks due to his speed and mobility, making him an ideal choice for targeting weak enemies in the background.
hero.hero_weakness:
1. **Weak Defense:** 
Lancelot is considered a relatively fragile hero, as his defense is low compared to other heroes. If he cannot avoid attacks, he can be defeated quickly. 

2. **Skill-based:** 
Lancelot needs to use his skills accurately and effectively, and if he mistimes or misfires, he may lose his effectiveness in battle. 

3. **Difficulty in controlling for beginners:** 
Because of his reliance on fast movement and successive skills, Lancelot may be difficult to control for new players or those who are not accustomed to fast heroes. 

4. **Weakness against heroes with crowd control:** 
Heroes with crowd control abilities (such as freezing or blocking) can hinder Lancelot's movement and limit his effectiveness.

data.recommend: One of the best heroes who play in tournaments and other things

spell.spell_desc: **Jungle** (Retribution) is a spell dedicated to **Jungle** heroes in **Mobile Legends**. It allows you to deal direct damage to jungle monsters, helping you kill them quickly and accumulate gold and experience faster. It can be upgraded later to provide additional effects such as slowing enemies, increasing damage, or restoring life. This spell is ideal for heroes who are tasked with killing monsters and controlling large map objectives such as Lord and Turtle.

hero.hero_overview:
Lancelot
Blade of Roses Prodigal son and genius swordsman of House Baroque.

As the eldest son of Duke Baroque, Lancelot was bom rebellious. Dead set on resisting the political marriage arranged for him by his father, he traveled the country and led a licentious life instead. After being wounded at a drunken brawl in a tavern. Lancelot was rescued by Odette and finally came to realize what his heart truly yearned for. The prodigal son made a repentant return and thus began his adventure across the Land of Dawn.
Odette lovers. and guinvere relatives.
hero.hero_strength:
passive Each time Lancelot charges, his damage is increased by 7.5% for 4 s, up to 30%.
skill 1 Lancelot charges in a designated direction, dealing 100 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies along the way and applying a Sword Mark to the first unmarked enemy hit. The Sword Mark lasts 5s.
If Lancelot successfully applies a Sword Mark to an enemy, the cooldown of this skill will reset.
skill 2 Lancelot strikes in the target direction 3 times, each time dealing 225 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit (damage is reduced when multiple enemies are hit). He's untargetable and invincible during this process. Enemies in the center of the area take all the 3 hits and are slowed by 20% for 0.5s. The Slowing Effect can stack.
This skill only benefits from 40% of Lancelot's Spell Vamp.
ultimate After a short period of charging, Lancelot performs an executioner's strike in a forward direction, dealing 400 (+150% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. He is invincible throughout the process.
hero.hero_weakness:
Chou
A good Chou player can counter almost any hero that the Land of Dawn has to offer.

When it comes to Lancelot specifically, Chou has a few abilities up his sleeve that can annoy any Lancelot players. The assassin is a very squishy hero, and one combo of Jeet Kune Do and The Way of Dragon usually gets the job done.
